question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open exactly maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection (default 5) long-lived streaming gRPC calls per TCP connection and then induce a network partition by dropping ACKs, causing all 5 slots to remain occupied indefinitely and starving all other users on that connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open many TCP connections each saturating maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots, then simulate a network partition by silently dropping packets, causing the applicationTaskExecutor thread pool to fill with blocked threads waiting for network I/O that never completes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of an idle timeout or flow-control deadline in grpcServerConfigurer() to hold open a gRPC streaming call across a simulated network partition, preventing the server from reclaiming the executor thread assigned to that call?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition scenario by establishing a gRPC call, then pausing TCP-level ACKs (e.g., via firewall rule or NAT timeout), causing the Netty server to buffer unbounded outbound data in memory because no maxConnectionAge or keepAlive is configured in grpcServerConfigurer()?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by opening connections up to the server's accept backlog, holding them in a half-open TCP state, and exploiting the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() sets no maxConnectionIdle, so the server never proactively closes idle half-open connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the applicationTaskExecutor thread pool by opening many connections each with maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection active calls, then inducing a network partition so threads block on write, effectively partitioning the server from all legitimate clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a maxConnectionAge setting in grpcServerConfigurer() to maintain a gRPC connection across a network partition indefinitely, preventing the server from load-balancing or rerouting traffic to healthy replicas?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of keepAlive configuration in grpcServerConfigurer() so that during a network partition the server never detects dead connections, causing executor threads to remain allocated to zombie calls and degrading service for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by sending a gRPC request that triggers a long-running database read via the readOnly TransactionTemplate, then dropping the network mid-transaction, leaving the DB connection held open and unavailable to other callers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate by initiating many concurrent gRPC calls that each open a DB transaction, then inducing a network partition between the gRPC server and the database, causing all transactions to hang and exhaust the DB connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection=5 limit by opening many connections and filling all slots with slow-consumer streaming calls, then inducing a network partition that prevents the server from sending RST frames, effectively locking all slots across all connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the shared applicationTaskExecutor: if the executor has a bounded queue and all threads are blocked on partitioned network writes, new legitimate gRPC calls are rejected with RejectedExecutionException, effectively partitioning all users from the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxConnectionAgeGrace in grpcServerConfigurer() so that during a network partition, graceful shutdown of connections never completes, causing the server to stall during rolling restarts or failover events?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition outside design parameters by sending a large number of gRPC streaming subscriptions that each hold a Netty channel, then dropping the network, causing Netty's write buffer to grow unboundedly since no flowControl or writeBufferWaterMark is configured?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure permitKeepAliveWithoutCalls, so that during a network partition the server drops keepAlive pings from legitimate clients, causing those clients to believe the server is unreachable and triggering unnecessary reconnection storms?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by holding maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots open on many connections and then inducing a TCP RST storm, causing the Netty event loop to be overwhelmed processing error events and starving legitimate connection handling?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a per-call deadline enforcement in grpcServerConfigurer() to keep a gRPC call alive across a network partition indefinitely, preventing the server from reclaiming resources associated with that call?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by exploiting the shared applicationTaskExecutor: by flooding the server with gRPC calls that each block on a partitioned downstream resource, the executor queue fills and the server becomes unable to process any new requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of maxInboundMessageSize configuration in grpcServerConfigurer() to send oversized gRPC frames during a network partition scenario, causing Netty to buffer large partial frames in memory and triggering an OOM that partitions the server from all clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of handshakeTimeout in grpcServerConfigurer() to initiate many TLS/gRPC handshakes and then drop the network mid-handshake, causing the server to hold handshake state and threads for an unbounded duration?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by opening connections up to the OS-level accept backlog, then inducing a partition so the server's Netty acceptor loop blocks on accept(), preventing new legitimate connections from being established?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection is applied per-connection but there is no global connection limit configured, allowing the attacker to open an unbounded number of connections each with 5 active calls, exhausting server resources during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of keepAliveTime and keepAliveTimeout in grpcServerConfigurer() so that during a network partition, the server never sends HTTP/2 PING frames to detect dead connections, causing zombie connections to accumulate and exhaust the executor?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition outside design parameters by subscribing to a long-lived gRPC topic stream, then inducing a network partition between the gRPC server and its Redis listener, causing the server to buffer all undelivered messages in memory until OOM?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate by initiating a gRPC call that opens a DB transaction, then inducing a network partition between the gRPC server and the DB, causing the transaction to hang indefinitely and hold a DB connection that is never released?
